recommendations: Cure bulbs under shade for 10-12 days; maintain neck tightness before storage.
Stack in ventilated racks or perforated crates to avoid moisture pockets.
Keep storage temperature between 25-30C with 65-70 percent relative humidity.
Avoid water contact after curing to prevent fungal rot.
Harvest at breaker stage for distant markets and shade-pack within 4 hours.
Use plastic crates with soft liners to reduce bruising during transport.
Hydrocool with chlorinated water (100 ppm) to extend shelf life by 2-3 days.
Stack boxes up to five layers to maintain ventilation in transit.
Pre-cool bunches to 2-4C before loading in reefer trucks for export routes.
Maintain sulphur dioxide pads inside boxes to control fungal growth.
Use food grade wax coating for domestic shipments beyond 400 km.
Adopt cluster bagging 20 days pre-harvest to reduce pest blemishes.
